hypothesis: Short-term price trends persist long enough that a fast moving average crossing above a slow moving average signals sustained upward momentum. The signal captures trend initiation before full mean reversion kicks in.
universe: Broad ETFs (SPY, QQQ) or liquid large-cap equities
entry_logic: Go long when fast SMA crosses above slow SMA. Go flat (or short) when fast crosses below.
exit_logic: Reverse signal (cross in opposite direction). Optional: trailing stop at 2x ATR.
position_sizing: Equal weight across universe. Or volatility-adjusted (inverse ATR weighting).
risk_management: Max drawdown stop: exit all if portfolio drawdown exceeds 15%. No single position > 20% of portfolio.
known_failure_modes: Choppy, sideways markets generate excessive whipsaws and transaction costs.
Underperforms during V-shaped reversals where the cross signal is too slow.
Parameter sensitivity: results vary significantly with window lengths, suggesting overfitting risk.

historical_performance.notes: Marginal alpha on SPY alone. Better on a diversified ETF basket. Mainly useful as a baseline.

references: quantopian/research_public - Lecture: Introduction to Trading Algorithms
